fin.general_ledger.cap_name: General Ledger and Chart of Accounts

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper ledger book
entry.example: Hand-written entries in a physical accounts book. Common for hawkers, family-run F&B, and very small services businesses that have not yet appointed an external bookkeeper.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Single-tab Excel GL
entry.example: Excel sheet with debit / credit columns and a running balance, often emailed to an external bookkeeper monthly for review. Typical for sole proprietorships and pre-revenue SMEs.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting
established.example: Xero, QuickBooks Online, or AutoCount Cloud. Bank-feed integration auto-imports transactions; user categorises in the UI; reports run on-demand. PSG-grant-eligible for most SG SMEs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated ERP GL
integrated.example: NetSuite, Sage Intacct, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. Multi-entity consolidation, multi-currency, audit trail across all sub-ledgers. Typical for mid-market SG SMEs with international operations.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Hand-written invoices + paper receipts
entry.example: Carbon-copy invoice book or printed Word-doc invoices, with payment ticked off in a physical ledger. Cash and PayNow receipts captured on receipt slips.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel AR tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet listing invoices with paid / unpaid columns, aging buckets calculated manually. Owner sends reminder emails by hand.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud AR with bank reconciliation
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks AR module. Auto-matches PayNow / bank transfers to invoices, sends automated reminders, surfaces aging dashboard.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated AR with credit + dunning automation
integrated.example: NetSuite / Sage Intacct AR. Credit-limit checks block order entry; multi-step dunning workflows escalate to collections; DSO reports tied to sales-commission calcs.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper bill pile + manual GL posting
entry.example: Vendor invoices arrive by post or email PDF, sit in a tray, and get hand-entered into the bookkeeper's monthly summary. Common for SMEs with low vendor count + cash-led ops.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel bills tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet with vendor, bill date, amount, due date, paid flag. Owner manually flags overdue bills weekly. Common stopgap between paper and cloud accounting.

established.name: ai_extract
established.label: Cloud AP with AI invoice OCR
established.example: Cloud accounting AP (Xero, QuickBooks) paired with AI invoice OCR (Dext, Hubdoc, Veryfi). Photograph or forward a PDF; AI extracts line items, vendor, amount, GST; user reviews and posts. Often the first AI workflow SMEs adopt.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated AP with PO matching
integrated.example: NetSuite / Sage Intacct AP. Three-way match between PO, goods-received, and invoice; multi-step approval routing by amount + cost-centre; vendor portal for self-service status.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper bank book + cash log
entry.example: Physical bank passbook updated weekly; cash float counted at shift end and noted in a logbook. Common for hawker stalls, small retail, single-outlet F&B.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel reconciliation sheet
entry.example: Download bank statement CSV, paste into Excel, match line items against GL transactions by date + amount. Manual but structured. Used by many SMEs pre-Xero.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting bank feed
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks / AutoCount Cloud connects directly to DBS, OCBC, UOB, Maybank, Standard Chartered SG via Plaid / Tink-equivalent feeds. Auto-imports transactions; user categorises with one-click rules.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Treasury management with multi-bank
integrated.example: NetSuite, Kyriba, or Sage Intacct treasury. Multi-bank cash pooling, FX exposure dashboards, automated payment runs via host-to-host bank integration. Typical for SG SMEs with multi-entity overseas operations.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: PDF invoices via email
entry.example: Invoices generated in Word / Excel, exported to PDF, emailed to customers. Common starting point; not InvoiceNow-compliant but still widespread among very small SMEs.

established.name: api
established.label: InvoiceNow via cloud accounting
established.example: IMDA IRSP-listed cloud accounting (Xero SG, QuickBooks SG, ABSS, AutoCount, Million). PEPPOL-compliant e-invoices sent via the access point automatically when "send via InvoiceNow" is ticked. PSG-eligible.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP-native PEPPOL access point
integrated.example: NetSuite, SAP Business One, or Sage Intacct with native PEPPOL access-point integration (often via Storecove / Tickstar providers). Multi-entity routing, custom UBL field mappings, full audit trail for IRAS GST reporting.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Manual Excel reports
entry.example: Bookkeeper compiles P&L and Balance Sheet monthly in Excel from GL transactions. PDF emailed to owner + tax agent. Standard practice for very small SMEs pre-cloud accounting.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting reports
established.example: Xero, QuickBooks, AutoCount Cloud — built-in P&L, Balance Sheet, cash-flow, and aging reports run on-demand. Custom report packs configurable per user role. PSG-eligible.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP financial analytics
integrated.example: NetSuite SuiteAnalytics, Sage Intacct Dimensions, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central Power BI. Multi-entity consolidation, drill-down to source transactions, scheduled board-pack delivery.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: Manual monthly journal entry
entry.example: Bookkeeper computes monthly payroll totals (gross, CPF, taxes, net) and manually keys the journal entry into Xero or AutoCount each month. Common when payroll is run in a separate (non-integrated) system.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud HRIS → cloud accounting export
established.example: Talenox / HReasily / Payboy generates a Xero / QuickBooks journal export after each pay run. One-click import to the accounting system, GL accounts pre-mapped. The default SG SME setup.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP-embedded payroll
integrated.example: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll → SAP S/4HANA GL, or NetSuite SuitePeople. Payroll JEs hit GL real-time, no export step, full cost-centre allocation. Common for mid-market SG SMEs with multi-entity structures.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: Phone contacts and WhatsApp
entry.example: Customer info lives in the founder's phone contacts + WhatsApp chat history. Pricing quoted ad-hoc; no structured record of past quotes.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Customer Excel / Google Sheet
entry.example: Shared Google Sheet listing customer name, contact, last purchase. Updated by sales staff manually when they remember.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CRM (free / low tier)
established.example: HubSpot Free, Zoho CRM, or Pipedrive. Contact + activity timeline, email tracking, basic pipelines. Often the first SaaS subscription after accounting.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise CRM with hierarchies
integrated.example: Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE, or HubSpot Pro+. Account hierarchies (parent / child / division), territory management, integrated marketing automation. Also adopted by small-but-sales-led businesses (6-15 person B2B consultancies, recruitment agencies) that skip the cloud-CRM tier and go straight here because pipeline visibility is load-bearing.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Notebook + business cards
entry.example: Leads scribbled in a notebook after networking events; cards stored in a Rolodex / business card holder. Common for owner-operator services businesses, B2B trades.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Lead Excel tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet with columns for source, contact, status (cold / warm / hot), next-action date. Often shared via Google Sheets when there are 2-3 sales staff.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CRM lead pipeline
established.example: HubSpot Free, Pipedrive, Zoho CRM. Web-to-lead forms, basic lead scoring, automated email nurture sequences. Standard kit for B2B SMEs with 5+ sales staff.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Marketing automation + lead scoring
integrated.example: Salesforce + Pardot / Marketing Cloud, or HubSpot Marketing Pro+. Multi-touch attribution, predictive lead scoring, integrated SDR-to-AE handoff workflows. Typical for SaaS / B2B SMEs with dedicated marketing team.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Word-doc quotes + email orders
entry.example: Quotes drafted in Word from a template, emailed to customer, orders confirmed by email reply. Order list maintained mentally or on a paper pad. Common for trades, consultancies, one-off project work.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel quote + order book
entry.example: Excel template with line items + auto-totals; orders tracked in a separate sheet with status column. Manual handoff to accounting for invoicing.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud quoting + order tool
established.example: Xero quotes, QuickBooks estimates, QuoteWerks, or Better Proposals. Branded PDF output, quote-to-invoice conversion one-click, optional e-signature. Integrated with cloud accounting AR.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: CPQ + ERP order management
integrated.example: NetSuite Order Mgmt, Salesforce CPQ, or SAP Business One Sales. Rule-based pricing + discount approval workflows, product configurator for bundled SKUs, real-time inventory allocation on order entry.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: WhatsApp + memory
entry.example: Sales staff track deals in their head + WhatsApp threads with customers. Owner asks "what's in the pipeline?" verbally at weekly meetings. Workable below 10-15 active deals.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel deals tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet with company, contact, value, stage, next-step, close-date columns. Updated weekly before sales meetings. Common pre-CRM stage; struggles past 3+ sales staff.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CRM deal pipeline
established.example: HubSpot Free / Pipedrive / Zoho CRM deal stages with drag-and-drop kanban, automated stage probabilities, win/loss reasons. Standard for B2B SMEs with 5+ sales staff.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise CRM with forecasting
integrated.example: Salesforce Sales Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Sales, HubSpot Sales Pro. Weighted forecasting, territory management, sales-leader dashboards, AI-assisted deal insights (Einstein, Sales Copilot).

entry.name: manual
entry.label: Email + memory
entry.example: Sales activity lives in personal Outlook / Gmail inboxes. Owner asks "did you follow up with X?" — answer comes from mental recall. No structured record.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel activity log
entry.example: Sales staff log calls / emails / meetings to a shared sheet, one row per interaction. Date + customer + summary columns. Often abandoned within 3 months due to friction.

established.name: api
established.label: CRM activity timeline + email tracking
established.example: HubSpot Free / Pipedrive auto-logs emails via browser extension + tracks open / click events. Manual call logging via mobile app. Standard mid-tier.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise CRM with auto-capture
integrated.example: Salesforce + Inbox / Outlook Connector, HubSpot Pro+ with conversation intelligence (Gong, Chorus). Automatic email + calendar sync, call recording transcription, AI-generated summaries surface in CRM timeline.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Physical stock cards
entry.example: Pen-and-paper stock cards stuck on shelves; manual cycle counts at month-end. Typical for very small retail / F&B with low SKU count.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel SKU register
entry.example: Excel sheet with SKU, opening qty, in, out, closing qty columns. Staff updates after each delivery / sale. Reconciliation gaps are common.

established.name: barcode
established.label: POS-driven barcode inventory
established.example: POS terminal (Vend, Square, EposNow) auto-decrements stock on each sale; goods-in scanned via handheld terminal. Real-time qty across one or two outlets.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: WMS / ERP-integrated stock
integrated.example: NetSuite, SAP Business One, or a dedicated WMS (Manhattan, Blue Yonder Lite). Multi-warehouse, bin / lot / serial tracking, real-time stock across e-commerce + POS + B2B channels via API.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper SKU list / printed catalogue
entry.example: Printed price list / product catalogue used as the source of truth; updated by reprint when prices change. Common for F&B menus and small retail.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel item catalogue
entry.example: Excel sheet with SKU, name, description, cost, list price, category. Often emailed to procurement / sales for reference. Updated when new SKUs are added.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud inventory item master
established.example: Zoho Inventory, Vend, AutoCount Cloud, or Cin7 Core. SKU with variants (size / colour / unit), barcode, supplier link, re-order point. Often synced to e-commerce (Shopify, Lazada) and POS.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: PIM + ERP-integrated item master
integrated.example: NetSuite or SAP Business One item master, or a dedicated PIM (Akeneo, Pimcore). Multi-attribute schemas, multi-currency pricing, BOM relationships, where-used queries, channel-specific variants.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper PO pad / email PO
entry.example: Three-ply paper PO booklet (top copy to supplier, second to warehouse, third filed). Or a Word doc / WhatsApp message sent to supplier. Common for small trades + project work.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel PO log
entry.example: Excel sheet logging PO number, supplier, items, total, status (sent / received / paid). Manual reconciliation against supplier invoice + bank payment.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting PO module
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks / AutoCount Cloud PO module. PO emailed from the system as PDF; convert PO → bill on goods-received; basic supplier history.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP P2P with vendor portals
integrated.example: NetSuite Procurement, SAP Business One P2P, or a dedicated P2P platform (Coupa, Ariba SMB). Multi-step approval routing, vendor self-service portal, blanket POs, three-way match tied to AP.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Rolodex / printed supplier list
entry.example: Business cards in a Rolodex or stapled supplier list in a binder. Owner phones suppliers when they need to reorder. Common in older SG trading firms.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel vendor master
entry.example: Excel sheet with vendor name, UEN, contact, bank account, payment terms, GST registration. Often shared with finance for AP processing. Manual maintenance.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting vendor records
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks / AutoCount vendor master with linked invoices, payment history, GST defaults. SG-compliant (UEN field, GST registration capture).

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP vendor portal with onboarding
integrated.example: NetSuite / SAP Business One / Coupa with vendor self-service portal. Vendors update their own banking + tax info, documents (W-9 equivalent, tax certificates) attached, approval workflow before activation.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel month-end reports
entry.example: Procurement / inventory metrics compiled manually each month — top SKUs by revenue, slow-movers, stock-out incidents. Static PDF emailed to management. Snapshot only.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud inventory analytics
established.example: Zoho Inventory / Cin7 / AutoCount Cloud built-in dashboards — inventory turns, ABC analysis, reorder alerts, GMROI. Live data, mobile-accessible.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP analytics with multi-warehouse
integrated.example: NetSuite SuiteAnalytics, SAP Business One Crystal Reports, Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM Power BI. Multi-warehouse + multi- entity rollups, lead-time variability analysis, supplier performance scorecards.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Physical HR folders
entry.example: Manila folders per employee with photocopied NRIC, work permit (if applicable), and signed offer letter. Locked in the owner's drawer; no digital backup.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: HR master Excel
entry.example: Single Excel sheet listing name, NRIC, role, start date, basic salary. Updated when hires / leavers happen. Used as the source for monthly payroll prep.

established.name: api
established.label: SG-local cloud HRIS
established.example: Talenox, HReasily, or Payboy. Employee self-service for leave / claims, integrated payroll with CPF / IRAS submissions, contract templates. Designed for SG SME statutory needs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS
integrated.example: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, or BambooHR (mid-market). Performance reviews, succession planning, learning & development modules; integrates with finance and headcount planning.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper leave forms
entry.example: Pre-printed leave application form filled in, signed by manager, filed in HR. Annual entitlement tracked manually on a wall chart or in HR's Excel.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel leave tracker
entry.example: Shared spreadsheet with employee × month grid; cells coloured for AL / MC / unpaid. Manual balance calculation. Workable for small headcounts but error-prone.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud HRIS leave module
established.example: Talenox, HReasily, Payboy, or QuickHR leave module. Employee applies via mobile app or browser; manager approves; balance auto-decrements. Statutory leave (AL / MC / parental) with SG defaults built in.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS with policy engine
integrated.example: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, BambooHR Pro. Configurable leave policies (tenure-based accruals, regional variations, carry-forward rules), integration with time / attendance and payroll, real-time absence dashboards.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Hand-calculated payroll
entry.example: Owner / bookkeeper calculates monthly pay + CPF on a calculator, writes payslip on a pre-printed slip, hands out cash or schedules bank transfer manually. Rare now, but still seen in very small family businesses.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel payroll with manual CPF
entry.example: Excel payroll template with formulas for OW / AW, employer + employee CPF, CDAC / SINDA / MBMF. Manual upload to CPF e-submission portal. Common for very small SMEs not yet on a payroll SaaS.

established.name: api
established.label: SG-local cloud payroll
established.example: Talenox, HReasily, Payboy, or QuickHR payroll. Auto-calculates CPF contributions, generates IR8A / Appendix 8A / IR21, submits CPF / IRAS electronically, integrates with SG bank GIRO payroll batches.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS payroll
integrated.example: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll, or ADP. Multi-country payroll engine, integration with finance + time + benefits, custom pay components for variable pay / commission / equity plans.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper sign-in sheet
entry.example: Physical sign-in sheet at office entry; staff write name + time when arriving / leaving. Manual reconciliation against payroll. Common in very small offices + retail outlets.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel self-reported timesheet
entry.example: Staff submit weekly Excel timesheets to HR via email. Manual aggregation for payroll. Workable up to ~15 staff.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud HRIS time module + biometric
established.example: Talenox / HReasily / Payboy time module with mobile clock-in (GPS-tagged) or wall-mounted biometric (fingerprint / face) device pushing to cloud. Auto-integrates with payroll.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise time + attendance
integrated.example: Workday Time Tracking, SAP SuccessFactors Time Mgmt, Kronos / UKG. Complex shift patterns, overtime rule engines, unionised workforce support, integration with payroll + finance cost centres.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Manually written payslips
entry.example: Pre-printed payslip booklet filled in by hand or basic Word- doc payslip printed per employee. Common in family-run SMEs with very small headcount.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel template payslip
entry.example: Excel payslip template with formulas; one printed PDF per employee per month, emailed or printed for collection. Compliant with MOM payslip requirements if all mandatory fields are included.

established.name: api
established.label: SG cloud payroll auto-generated payslip
established.example: Talenox / HReasily / Payboy generates MOM-compliant payslips (gross, deductions, net, leave balance, YTD) auto-emailed to employees in PDF. Mobile-app accessible.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS payslip portal
integrated.example: Workday / SAP SuccessFactors / BambooHR employee self-service portal with payslip history, year-end IR8A, leave balance, benefits statements. Multi-language, multi-country.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper checklist + form-filling
entry.example: New hire fills paper forms (contact, bank, CPF election, IC copy). Manager runs a printed onboarding checklist (laptop setup, ID card, intro emails). Manual offboarding equivalent.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud HRIS onboarding workflow
established.example: Talenox / HReasily / BambooHR Lite onboarding — digital contract signing (DocuSign / SignNow integration), employee self-service profile setup, document upload, automated first-day email sequences.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise onboarding with IT provisioning
integrated.example: Workday + Okta / Azure AD integration. Hire creates AD account → email account → Slack / Microsoft 365 access → laptop ticket → payroll record auto-provisioned. Offboarding revokes all access in one trigger.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper asset list + physical tags
entry.example: Asset inventory in a binder; each asset has a hand-written number sticker. Common for small workshops, gyms, F&B with a dozen tagged items (fryers, ovens, equipment).

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel asset register
entry.example: Excel sheet with asset code, description, purchase date, cost, depreciation method, current location. Maintained by finance for tax / audit purposes; often disconnected from ops use of the assets.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CMMS / asset management
established.example: Limble, UpKeep, Fiix, or MaintainX. Asset tree with parent / child relationships, QR-code labels for mobile scanning, work-order history per asset. Designed for SME maintenance teams.

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: EAM with RFID + IoT condition monitoring
integrated.example: IBM Maximo, SAP EAM, or Infor EAM with RFID asset tags and connected sensors (vibration, temperature, runtime hours). Predictive maintenance triggers, real-time condition dashboards. Typical for capital-intensive SG SMEs in manufacturing / logistics.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper job sheet
entry.example: Maintenance request raised as a paper job card; technician carries it to the asset, fills in completion notes, returns to office for filing. Common in older SG manufacturing + facilities ops.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel work order log
entry.example: Office logs maintenance requests in Excel with asset, issue, assignee, status. WhatsApp used to assign to techs. Workable for ~12-15 assets and weekly cadence.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CMMS work orders
established.example: Limble / UpKeep / Fiix / MaintainX work orders with mobile app for technicians, photo attachment, QR-code asset scan, parts used logging. PSG-eligible for some.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise EAM work orders
integrated.example: IBM Maximo, Infor EAM, SAP Plant Maintenance. Multi-craft scheduling, permit-to-work integration, MRO inventory linkage, condition-based work order triggers from IoT sensor data.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Wall calendar with PM dates
entry.example: Physical calendar in maintenance office with PM dates per asset marked manually. Reminders set on phones. Common for small workshops with <20 assets.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel PM schedule
entry.example: Excel sheet per asset with PM type, frequency, last-done, next-due. Manual conditional formatting flags overdue items. Common SG manufacturer baseline.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CMMS PM module
established.example: Limble / UpKeep / Fiix preventive maintenance module — schedule by time / usage / meter reading, auto-generates work orders, mobile reminders to technicians. Asset hierarchy aware.

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: Predictive maintenance from IoT
integrated.example: IBM Maximo Predict, GE APM, SAP PdMS. Vibration / temperature / runtime-hours sensors trigger PM proactively. Machine learning models predict failure 7-30 days ahead. Typical for capital-intensive SG manufacturers (semiconductor, pharma).

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel parts list
entry.example: Excel sheet listing spare part, qty on hand, location, asset compatibility. Reorder triggered manually when stock looks low. Common for small workshops.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CMMS parts module
established.example: Limble / UpKeep / Fiix integrated parts inventory. Auto- decrements on work-order consumption, re-order point alerts, supplier link, mobile-scan during job.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated EAM + ERP inventory
integrated.example: IBM Maximo + ERP item master, or SAP PM + SAP MM. MRO inventory treated as part of broader inventory with same procurement, cycle counting, and obsolescence policies. Spare-parts kitting for planned shutdowns.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper job sheets
entry.example: Carbon-copy job sheet pad; technician keeps top copy, customer signs second, office files third. Common for trades, repair shops, small contractors.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: WhatsApp + Excel job log
entry.example: Office WhatsApps job details to technician; technician sends photo of completed job back. Office logs everything in Excel for invoicing. Surprisingly common in SG trades.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud FSM platform
established.example: Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceM8, or Fergus. Mobile app for technicians, GPS dispatch, quote / invoice generation, payment collection on-site. Built for sub-50-tech home services businesses.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise FSM with integrations
integrated.example: ServiceMax, Salesforce Field Service, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Field Service, or IFS FSM. Integration with CRM / ERP / inventory, complex scheduling optimisation, contract + warranty handling, customer self-service portal.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: WhatsApp + phone dispatch
entry.example: Office WhatsApps jobs to technician groups; first to respond takes the job. Owner uses memory of who's where. Common for sub-5-technician SG aircon / plumbing / electrical firms.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel job board / scheduling
entry.example: Excel schedule per day with technician × time-slot grid. Manually assigned + colour-coded. Printed daily; updated via WhatsApp during the day.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud FSM dispatch
established.example: Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceM8 drag-and-drop scheduling + GPS view of technicians. Customer-facing booking link (e.g. Jobber Online Booking). Standard kit for SG home services SMEs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise FSM route optimisation
integrated.example: ServiceMax, Salesforce Field Service, MS Dynamics 365 FS with auto-scheduling engines that optimise routes for travel time + technician skills + SLA. Real-time re-optimisation when jobs slip.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel technician roster
entry.example: Excel sheet listing technicians, skills, certifications, contact, vehicle assignment, availability. Office references when dispatching jobs.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud FSM technician profile
established.example: Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceM8 technician profiles with skills, certifications, photo, GPS-trackable status. Used by dispatch logic to filter eligible techs per job.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise FSM with skills matrix
integrated.example: ServiceMax / Salesforce Field Service skills matrix (certification expiry tracking, license matching per job type, training-record integration). Critical for regulated industries (medical, fire-safety, utilities).

entry.name: manual
entry.label: WhatsApp + phone calls
entry.example: Field comms via WhatsApp groups. Tech sends "started" / "finished" + photo of completed work + customer signature on paper. Workable but no structured data flow back to office.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud FSM mobile app
established.example: Jobber Mobile, Housecall Pro Mobile, ServiceM8 mobile app. Job details, customer info, photo / signature capture, parts / time logging, on-site payment collection (Stripe / PayNow QR). Works offline + syncs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise FSM mobile (offline + AR)
integrated.example: ServiceMax Go, Salesforce Field Service Mobile, MS Dynamics 365 FS Mobile. Offline-first architecture for poor-coverage sites, AR-assisted repair guidance, parts barcode scanning, knowledge-base search.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper logbook in each vehicle
entry.example: Physical logbook in each van / truck for driver to record mileage, fuel, maintenance. Common for small fleets (< 5 vehicles) — trades, food delivery, school transport.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel fleet master
entry.example: Excel sheet listing each vehicle by plate, year, model, COE expiry, road tax expiry, insurance renewal, last service date. Manual reminders / calendar entries for renewals.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud fleet management
established.example: Fleetio, AUTOsist, or Whip Around. Web + mobile app, mileage logging, maintenance scheduling, expiry alerts, fuel tracking. Some integrations with telematics dongles for basic GPS.

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: Telematics + IoT fleet platform
integrated.example: Geotab, Samsara, Verizon Connect, or Cartrack. Real-time GPS, driver behaviour scoring (harsh braking / speeding), predictive maintenance from OBD diagnostics, integration with fuel cards + dashcams. Standard for SG logistics / delivery SMEs > 20 vehicles.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper driver file + license copies
entry.example: Physical folder per driver with NRIC / work permit / driving licence copies, vocational licence renewal dates tracked on a wall calendar. Common for small SG logistics firms (< 10 drivers).

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel driver roster
entry.example: Excel sheet listing drivers, IC, licence class + expiry, vehicle assignment, contact, attendance / leave status. Manual licence-renewal reminders.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud fleet driver module
established.example: Fleetio / Whip Around driver profiles with document expiry alerts (licence, vocational, medical), assignment to vehicles, inspection history, mobile app self-service for DVIRs (Daily Vehicle Inspection Reports).

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: Telematics with driver behaviour scoring
integrated.example: Geotab / Samsara / Cartrack with driver-ID keys + behaviour scoring (harsh braking, speeding, idling, seatbelt use). Insurance discounts for fleets above safety thresholds. Standard for SG logistics > 20 drivers.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper fuel receipts
entry.example: Drivers collect printed fuel receipts (Shell, Caltex, Esso, SPC) + submit to office for monthly reimbursement. Reconciliation against mileage manual. Common for very small fleets.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel fuel log
entry.example: Excel sheet with date, vehicle, fuel litres, cost, odometer reading. Manual fuel-efficiency calculation. Workable up to ~10 vehicles.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud fleet + fuel card integration
established.example: Fleetio / Whip Around with Shell / Caltex / SPC fleet card integration — fuel transactions auto-imported. Per-vehicle MPG reports, exception alerts (unusual purchases).

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: Telematics fuel monitoring
integrated.example: Geotab / Samsara CAN-bus fuel-level monitoring direct from vehicle ECU. Anti-theft alerts on unusual fuel drops, fuel- slip detection (card use without vehicle present), engine idling time correlation.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Wall calendar + paper logbook
entry.example: Maintenance dates marked on wall calendar; per-vehicle paper logbook records services completed. Common for 2-5 vehicle fleets.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel maintenance schedule
entry.example: Excel sheet per vehicle with service intervals (km / time- based), last-service, next-due, supplier. Conditional formatting flags overdue. Office staff calls workshop.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud fleet maintenance
established.example: Fleetio / Whip Around maintenance scheduling — mileage-based + time-based triggers, mobile reminders, work-order generation, parts + labour cost tracking per vehicle.

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: Telematics-driven predictive maintenance
integrated.example: Geotab / Samsara reads OBD-II diagnostic codes from vehicle + auto-triggers maintenance based on real engine condition, not just mileage. Predicts likely failures from engine telemetry patterns.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper recipe / process card
entry.example: Hand-written or printed recipe card listing ingredients / components and quantities. Used by F&B kitchens, small food manufacturers, custom job shops.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel BOM
entry.example: Excel sheet listing parent SKU, components, quantities, unit cost. Often a separate file per finished good. Hard to maintain when revisions happen.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud manufacturing BOM
established.example: Katana, MRPeasy, Cin7 Core, or Odoo Manufacturing. Multi-level BOM with auto-cost rollup from component prices, revision history, simple where-used queries. Targeted at SG SME contract manufacturers + food producers.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP BOM with engineering change control
integrated.example: NetSuite Manufacturing, SAP Business One Production, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM. Phantom BOMs, by-products, alternates, formal ECN workflow, integration with CAD / PLM for engineered products.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper job card (traveller)
entry.example: Production job card travels with the part through the shop floor; each station's operator stamps + signs completion. Common in older SG precision shops + small custom job shops.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel work order log
entry.example: Excel sheet logging WO number, part, qty, status, machine assignment. Office prints daily WO list for shop-floor supervisor. Workable for ~15-20 active WOs.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud manufacturing WO
established.example: Katana, MRPeasy, Cin7 Core, Odoo Manufacturing work orders with status tracking (planned / in-progress / complete), material reservation, time + scrap logging. Mobile / tablet app on shop floor.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP WO with MES integration
integrated.example: NetSuite Manufacturing / SAP Business One Production / MS Dynamics 365 SCM work orders integrated with shop-floor MES (Plex, Aegis FactoryLogix, SAP DM). Real-time machine status, OEE tracking, scrap reasons coded.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Manual MRP in Excel
entry.example: Excel sheet calculates raw material requirements from forecast / order backlog. Per-SKU sheet with on-hand, allocated, on-order, requirements. Procurement raises POs manually from this output.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud manufacturing MRP
established.example: Katana / MRPeasy / Cin7 Core MRP runs nightly — generates PO + WO suggestions from sales orders + safety stock + lead times. One-click convert to PO. Suited to SG SME contract manufacturers.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP MRP + forecasting
integrated.example: NetSuite Advanced Manufacturing, SAP Business One MRP, Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM. Multi-level netting, forecast consumption, sourcing rules (alternates / multi-source), DDMRP-style buffer mgmt for high-mix shops.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Whiteboard with magnetic strips
entry.example: Production planner uses magnetic strips on whiteboard for each job × machine × shift. Re-arranges when priorities change. Tactile, fast for small shop, no audit trail.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel Gantt schedule
entry.example: Excel sheet with machine rows × time columns; cells filled with job codes. Conditional formatting for status. Updated daily; printed for shop floor.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud manufacturing scheduler
established.example: Katana / MRPeasy / Cin7 Core scheduling — drag-and-drop Gantt, capacity warnings when overloaded, mobile-view for floor leads. Good for SG SME contract manufacturers.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: APS with finite capacity
integrated.example: NetSuite Advanced Manufacturing + Preactor APS, SAP B1 + Planet Together, MS D365 SCM + Asprova. Finite-capacity algorithms optimise across machines + tools + labour + shifts; what-if scenarios; constraint visualisation.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Sticky notes / paper task list
entry.example: Whiteboard with sticky notes per task / per person, or a paper to-do list updated daily. Common for very small creative / services teams.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel / Google Sheets task tracker
entry.example: Spreadsheet with task, owner, status, due date columns. Often one tab per project. Workable up to maybe 5 active projects before maintenance friction kicks in.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud project tool
established.example: Trello, Asana, ClickUp, Notion, or Monday.com. Kanban / list / Gantt views, task dependencies, file attachments, notifications. The default first-SaaS-after-accounting for many service SMEs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: PPM / ERP project module
integrated.example: NetSuite OpenAir, Microsoft Project Online, Wrike Enterprise, or Smartsheet Advance. Resource capacity planning, portfolio dashboards, integration with timesheets / billing / finance. Typical for SG consultancies + agencies with 30+ staff billing on projects.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel project list
entry.example: Excel sheet listing active projects with code, client, PM, start date, end date, budget, status. Updated weekly at team meeting.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud project tool
established.example: Asana, ClickUp, Notion, Monday.com project records with hierarchy (portfolio → project → sub-project), client tagging, member assignments. Good for SG agencies + service firms < 50 staff.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: PPM with portfolio hierarchies
integrated.example: NetSuite OpenAir, Microsoft Project Online, Smartsheet Advance, Wrike Enterprise. Project / programme / portfolio hierarchies, custom fields by project type, integration with finance + resource planning.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper weekly timesheet
entry.example: Pre-printed weekly timesheet form filled in by hand; submitted to manager / PM for approval and to HR for payroll prep. Common in older services / consulting firms.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel timesheet template
entry.example: Excel template per week per employee; auto-totals by project / task. Emailed for approval. Workable but error- prone for billing reconciliation.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud time tracking
established.example: Toggl Track, Harvest, Clockify, Asana time tracking. Timer + manual entry, mobile app, project / task / billable flag. Reports export to billing software.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP-integrated timesheet
integrated.example: NetSuite OpenAir, MS Project Online, Salesforce PSA. Timesheets feed billing + WIP + revenue recognition + cost accounting automatically. Approval workflows configurable per project / client.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel time × rate → manual invoice
entry.example: PM exports time entries to Excel, multiplies by billable rate, copies into a Word invoice template, exports to PDF, emails customer. Manual handoff to accounting for AR.

established.name: api
established.label: Time tool + cloud accounting handoff
established.example: Harvest / Toggl → Xero / QuickBooks invoice creation (semi-automated). PM reviews + approves the draft invoice before send. Common SG consultancy setup.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: PSA automated billing
integrated.example: NetSuite OpenAir, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Project Operations, Salesforce PSA. Time + expense + milestones flow into auto- generated invoices on configurable cadence; revenue recognition computed in parallel.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper filing cabinet
entry.example: Physical filing cabinet with labelled folders for invoices, contracts, HR records. Common for any small business that predates the cloud era and hasn't migrated.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Shared cloud drive (personal-tier)
entry.example: Dropbox Personal, Google Drive personal, OneDrive, or a NAS shared between staff. Folders by department / project. Works but lacks access control, retention policies, audit.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud DMS (business tier)
established.example: Google Workspace, Microsoft 365 Business, or Dropbox Business. Per-user access, shared team drives, basic version control, mobile app. Standard kit for any SG SME with > 10 staff.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise DMS with retention + audit
integrated.example: SharePoint Online (with retention labels), Box, M-Files, or DocuWare. Records-management policies, version + check-in / check-out, audit trail of access, integration with ERP / CRM / HR for source-of-record linking.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: Email + WhatsApp approval chains
entry.example: Approval requests sent by email or WhatsApp; approver replies "ok" / "approved". No structured audit trail. Common for small SMEs with informal approval culture.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: SharePoint / Google Sheet with approval column
entry.example: Shared sheet with one row per request + a status / approver column. Manual updates by the approver. Workable but no notifications, no SLA tracking.

established.name: api
established.label: SaaS workflow tools
established.example: Zapier, Make (Integromat), Microsoft Power Automate, n8n. Visual workflow builder, trigger-action chains across SaaS apps, basic approval steps. Standard mid-tier for SG SMEs cobbling integrations.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP / CRM embedded workflow engine
integrated.example: NetSuite SuiteFlow, Salesforce Flow, MS Dynamics 365 Workflow, SAP Business Workflow. Native to the platform, field-level rules, multi-step approvals, escalation by SLA, role + condition-based branching.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: Email + WhatsApp groups
entry.example: Alerts pushed manually via email or WhatsApp groups. Common for any small SME. Risk: critical alerts get lost in chat noise; no acknowledgement tracking.

established.name: api
established.label: SaaS notification channels
established.example: Slack channels, Microsoft Teams alerts, Telegram bots configured to push from cloud apps (e.g. Xero error notifications, GitHub events, Stripe failures). Structured channels per concern.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP alert engine with subscription rules
integrated.example: NetSuite Saved-Search alerts, Salesforce Apex Triggers alerts, SAP Business Workflow notifications. User-defined subscription rules, escalation paths, multi-channel (email + SMS + in-app) with acknowledgement tracking.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper forms scanned to PDF
entry.example: Pre-printed forms (job application, customer feedback, quality inspection) filled in by hand. Scanned to PDF for email + filing. Common for compliance-driven SG industries.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Google Forms + sheet
entry.example: Free Google Forms or Microsoft Forms. Responses auto- populate a sheet for review. Workable for ad-hoc internal surveys, basic data capture.

established.name: api
established.label: SaaS form builder
established.example: Jotform, Typeform, Microsoft Forms (Pro), Zoho Forms. Conditional logic, payment collection, file uploads, integrations with Sheets / Slack / Mailchimp. SG-popular for booking forms, feedback surveys.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP-embedded form designer
integrated.example: ServiceNow Forms, Salesforce Lightning Forms, NetSuite Custom Forms, SAP Fiori app forms. Forms tied to platform objects (cases, contacts, work orders), trigger workflows on submission, audit-trail per field change.
